highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 upgrades to Skia m151 and delivers a sweep of allocation-free hot-path improvements.
highlights_body: This release eliminates several per-call heap allocations in HarfBuzzSharp and SKShaper, adds ReadOnlySpan overloads to SKColor hex parsing, and fixes a WASM build regression. It also removes the last unsafe modifier from GRContextOptions.

bullets.detail: The bundled Skia engine is now built from chrome/m151, bringing upstream rendering bug fixes and improvements across all platforms.

bullets.lead: WASM build: SK_BUILD_FOR_WASM redefinition warning fixed
bullets.detail: A macro redefinition in the Skia submodule caused a build warning (and potential errors) for WASM targets. The submodule has been bumped to resolve it.

bullets.lead: SKColor hex parsing with ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads
bullets.detail: SKColor.Parse and TryParse now accept ReadOnlySpan<char> in addition to string, avoiding string allocations in hot paths that parse color values.
The unnecessary unsafe modifier on GRContextOptions has been removed, cleaning up the public API surface without any behavioral change.
UTF-8 encoding is now done via a pooled buffer, eliminating the per-call byte array allocation when adding text to a HarfBuzz shaping buffer.

bullets.lead: HarfBuzzSharp.Tag.Parse and SKFourByteTag.Parse no longer allocate per call
bullets.detail: Both Parse methods previously allocated a char[4] on every call to read the four-byte tag. They now use stack memory, removing the allocation.

bullets.lead: SKShaper glyph results read via zero-copy spans
bullets.detail: Glyph result arrays from the native shaper are now accessed through zero-copy spans instead of marshalled copies, reducing allocation and improving throughput in text shaping.

contributor_summaries.ramezgerges: Golden-image test harness and Uno sample contributions

preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.2.1: Preview 2 delivered the full performance sweep: allocation-free HarfBuzz buffer encoding, zero-copy span shaper results, tag parse fixes, and SKColor span overloads. It also merged the second round of chrome/m151 upstream bug fixes.
preview_summaries.v4.151.0-preview.1.1: Preview 1 opened the 4.151 line with the Skia m151 milestone upgrade, the WASM submodule fix, and the GRContextOptions unsafe cleanup.

harfbuzz_summary: HarfBuzzSharp 14.2.1 carries two performance improvements in this release: allocation-free AddUtf8 encoding and a stack-allocated Tag.Parse, both eliminating per-call heap allocations in shaping hot paths.
